source.title: Chevron Richmond Refinery Pipe Rupture and Fire - Final Investigation Report

actions_taken: Attempted to install leak repair clamp
Attempted to remove insulation from leaking pipe
Developed new leak response protocol post-incident
event.summary: On August 6, 2012, a catastrophic rupture occurred in the 4-sidecut piping of the #4 Crude Unit at Chevron Richmond Refinery. The 52-inch component of the 8-inch line ruptured due to sulfidation corrosion, releasing approximately 10,800 barrels per day of light gas oil. The release vaporized into a large vapor cloud that engulfed 19 employees. Approximately two minutes after release, the vapor ignited, resulting in a large fire. One firefighter was caught in the fireball but escaped due to protective equipment. Six employees suffered minor injuries. Approximately 15,000 people from surrounding communities sought medical treatment. The incident resulted in a Community Warning System Level 3 alert and shelter-in-place advisory for Richmond, San Pablo, and North Richmond.
recommendations: Chevron to engage diverse team for documented damage mechanism hazard review as part of Process Hazard Analysis cycle
Include applicable industry best practices and inherently safer systems in hazard reviews
Report leading and lagging process safety indicators at all California Chevron refineries
Require documented use of inherently safer systems analysis and hierarchy of controls in establishing safeguards
California refineries to perform documented damage mechanism hazard reviews
Identify and require reporting of leading and lagging process safety indicators
Establish multi-agency process safety regulatory program for California oil refineries
EPA to jointly plan and conduct inspections with Cal/OSHA and state agencies
Enhance and restructure California's process safety management regulations for petroleum refineries
Implement compensation system to ensure regulator capability in process safety oversight

hazards.name: Sulfidation corrosion in carbon steel piping
Variable silicon content in carbon steel piping
High-temperature light gas oil in process piping

threats.name: Pipe wall thinning due to sulfidation corrosion
Accelerated corrosion in low-silicon carbon steel components
Undetected variation in silicon content across piping circuit
Catastrophic pipe rupture from wall thinning
Large vapor cloud formation from high-temperature release

consequences.name: Loss of containment of light gas oil
Fire and thermal injury to personnel
Off-site impact with community health effects

controls.name: Sulfidation Corrosion Inspection Program
controls.lod_basis: Inspection and monitoring to detect wall thinning before failure

supporting_text: Inspection data for the 4-sidecut piping, where measurements were historically taken on high-silicon fittings, indicated the 4-sidecut piping could safely operate through 2016
A Crude Unit metallurgical analysis recommendation to perform 100 percent volumetric inspection of the 4-sidecut line submitted for the 2007 turnaround was approved
the guided wave bracelets were only installed on a small portion of the 4-sidecut line which did not include the 52-inch component that ultimately failed

controls.name: 100 Percent Component Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Identification of low-silicon components susceptible to accelerated corrosion
controls.lod_basis: Comprehensive inspection to characterize silicon content and corrosion susceptibility

supporting_text: Chevron ETC metallurgists released within Chevron a formal report dated September 30, 2009 titled Updated Inspection Strategies for Preventing Sulfidation Corrosion Failures in Chevron Refineries (ETC Sulfidation Failure Prevention Initiative). The initiative specifically recommends that inspectors perform 100 percent component inspection on high-temperature carbon steel piping susceptible to sulfidation corrosion

The process conditions of the 4-sidecut stream placed it in the highest priority for 100 percent component inspection. However, the 4-sidecut piping was not 100 percent component inspected prior to the August 2012 incident

Post-incident testing of samples of the 4-sidecut piping from the Chevron Richmond Refinery identified silicon content ranging from 0.01 weight percent to 0.2 weight percent. Of 12 samples taken from the 8-inch and the adjacent 12-inch 4-sidecut line, six had a silicon concentration of less than 0.10 weight percent

controls.name: Material of Construction Upgrade to Higher Chromium Steel
controls.barrier_role: Elimination of sulfidation corrosion hazard through inherently safer design
controls.lod_basis: Inherently safer design using materials resistant to sulfidation corrosion

supporting_text: Steel alloys containing at least 9 weight percent chromium are more resistant to sulfidation corrosion than carbon steel and do not present the hazard of extreme variations in corrosion rates in components within the same piping circuit due to slight differences in chemical composition

Chevron did not effectively implement internal recommendations to help prevent pipe failures due to sulfidation corrosion. In the 10 years prior to the incident, a small number of Chevron personnel with knowledge and understanding of sulfidation corrosion recommended on several occasions either a one-time inspection of every component within the 4-sidecut piping circuit or an upgrade of the material of construction of the 4-sidecut piping
recommendations to replace the 8-inch 4-sidecut piping during the 2007 and 2011 turnarounds were denied in accordance with the Framing Document criteria

controls.name: Turnaround Planning and Work Scope Management
controls.barrier_role: Approval and execution of maintenance and replacement work
controls.lod_basis: Formal process to identify and approve critical maintenance work during planned turnarounds

supporting_text: The Framing Document sets the criteria for work items that can be automatically accepted as turnaround work items during the planned turnaround. Less urgent items and those that may be performed on the run (while the unit is operating) or during the next turnaround are not included by default in the turnaround work scope

The Sulfidation Failure Prevention Initiative developed by the ETC metallurgist experts was not considered a valid mandate for justifying turnaround work which otherwise fell outside the acceptance criteria of the Framing Document

no high-level manager was assigned responsibility to ensure that the ETC Sulfidation Failure Prevention Initiative or other ETC sulfidation recommendations were included in the turnaround scope, so all responsibility to implement the ETC recommendations was placed on lower-level employees, who did not have decision-making or funding authority

controls.name: Unit Reliability Improvement Process (URIP)
controls.barrier_role: Stewardship of mechanical reliability and implementation of technical recommendations
controls.lod_basis: Formal organizational process to manage reliability improvements and technical initiatives

supporting_text: Chevron relies on its Unit Reliability Improvement Process (URIP) and its associated programs, including Unit Reliability Briefs (URBs) and Reliability Steering Committee meetings, to steward mechanical reliability at its various refineries

Employees meeting within the various URIP programs discussed the ETC Sulfidation Failure Prevention Initiative. However, the metallurgical and inspection staff assigned by the URB and Reliability Steering Committee to implement the ETC Sulfidation Failure Prevention Initiative routed all recommendations through the turnaround planning process
no high-level refinery managers who attended URBs and Reliability Steering Committee meetings took or were assigned responsibility for the ETC Sulfidation recommendations

controls.name: Leak Detection and Response Protocol
controls.barrier_role: Rapid detection and mitigation of process leaks to prevent escalation
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response procedures to detect, contain, and suppress leaks

supporting_text: Leak Mitigation and Discovery Attempts Worsened Leak
Miscommunication regarding 4-sidecut properties
contractors erecting scaffolding beneath the leak location
firefighters attempting to remove the 4-sidecut insulation, the resulting fire, and fire extinguishing
Chevron's New Leak Response Protocol

controls.name: Area Control and Hazardous Area Assessment
controls.barrier_role: Identification and control of hazardous areas during leak response
controls.lod_basis: Procedures to assess and control hazardous areas to protect personnel

supporting_text: Area Control and Hazardous Area Assessment
The ruptured pipe released flammable, high temperature light gas oil, which then partially vaporized into a large, opaque vapor cloud that engulfed 19 Chevron U.S.A. Inc. (Chevron) employees

Eighteen of the employees safely escaped from the vapor cloud just before ignition; one employee, a Chevron refinery firefighter, was inside a fire engine that was caught within the fireball when the process fluid ignited

controls.name: Process Safety Management (PSM) Program
controls.barrier_role: Comprehensive management of process hazards and safeguards
controls.lod_basis: Formal PSM program to identify hazards and ensure effective safeguards

supporting_text: CHEVRON PROCESS SAFETY PROGRAMS
Chevron Corporation refinery process safety programs
Require that Process Hazard Analyses include documentation of the recognized methodologies, rationale and conclusions used to claim that safeguards intended to control hazards will be effective

controls.name: Mechanical Integrity Program
controls.barrier_role: Maintenance and inspection of equipment to ensure continued integrity
controls.lod_basis: Systematic inspection and maintenance program to prevent equipment failures

supporting_text: MECHANICAL INTEGRITY INDUSTRY STANDARD DEFICIENCIES
Chevron Minimum Pipe Thickness Program
Inspection Database report analyzed by unit inspectors when determining piping remaining life and when making piping replacement recommendations

controls.name: Minimum Pipe Thickness Program
controls.barrier_role: Monitoring of pipe wall thickness to detect corrosion damage
controls.lod_basis: Thickness measurement locations (TMLs) to track wall thinning over time

supporting_text: Chevron Minimum Pipe Thickness Program
To-scale schematic of 4-sidecut piping original wall thickness (0.322-inch), Minimum Alert Thickness (0.13-inch), and Minimal Required Thickness (0.11-inch) using API RP 574 default values
A 2011 effort added an additional 12 CML locations on straight-run piping components. A CML was not placed, however, on the low-silicon 52-inch component that failed on August 6, 2012

controls.name: Stop Work Authority (SWA)
controls.barrier_role: Authority for personnel to stop unsafe work
controls.lod_basis: Empowerment of all employees to halt operations when safety concerns are identified

supporting_text: Stop Work Authority
Chevron Richmond Refinery 2008 Safety Culture Survey responses to question of 'Stopping Unsafe Work' by operators and mechanics

controls.name: Community Warning System (CWS)
controls.barrier_role: Alert and notification of off-site population to hazardous release
controls.lod_basis: Emergency notification system to alert community and enable protective actions

supporting_text: This chain of events resulted in a Community Warning System (CWS) Level 3 alert, and a shelter-in-place advisory (SIP) was issued at 6:38 p.m. for the cities of Richmond, San Pablo, and North Richmond. It was lifted later that night, at 11:12 p.m., after the fire was fully under control
Contra Costa County Community Warning System

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
